header.title: Index - Laragon
menuprefs.title: Customize the menu
a11y.title: Accessibility options
readme.intro: Home dashboard for the www/ folder of your local server (Laragon / WAMP / MAMP). It lists your sites, creates projects and gathers handy dev tools. Local access only.
readme.f_listing: Automatically lists the sites and files in www/.
readme.f_create: Quickly create a new project (with an optional folder structure).
readme.f_lang: Multilingual interface: French, English, Spanish, Italian, German.
readme.f_security: Localhost-only access; logging of accesses and blocked attempts.
readme.u1: The page lists every site in www/. Type a name and click Create to scaffold a new project (optionally with a folder structure).
readme.u2: Open the side menu (≡, top-right) for the built-in tools: Adminer, connection logs, server info (phpinfo).
readme.u3: Access is restricted to localhost: from any other machine the dashboard simply returns a 404.
readme.f_a11y: Adjustable accessibility: light/dark theme, text size, high contrast, dyslexia font, colour-blindness modes, reduced motion.
readme.f_customize: Customizable side menu (enable/disable, choose which categories show).
readme.u4: Open Accessibility in the side menu (≡) to adjust the theme, text size, contrast and more — your preferences are saved in your browser.
readme.author: Built by Nicolas Degabriel —
learning.title: Learning Cycle
learning.intro: This pedagogical model describes the 4 progressive stages of learning, from the center outward:
learning.intro_perso: As a web development trainer, I designed this cycle to structure the way I teach — every skill is built by going through these four stages.
learning.imitate_desc: Observe and reproduce an existing model identically. This is the starting point: understanding by copying.
learning.adapt_desc: Modify the model to fit a different context. You begin to make the technique your own.
learning.transpose_desc: Apply the learned principles to an entirely new domain. The skill becomes transferable.
learning.transmit_desc: Teach others what you have mastered. This is the ultimate proof of understanding and the engine of the cycle.
folder.instruction: Create a new project in laragon/www/ :
folder.confirm_create: A folder named: {name} will be created!
folder.subfolder_tooltip: Enabled: creates a full directory structure (Assets, Includes, Uploads...). Disabled: creates only the folder with a simple index.php.
folder.invalid_name: Invalid folder name. Use only letters, digits, spaces, hyphens and underscores (no slashes or "..").
folder.structure_created: Structure created: Assets/ (Css, Js, Img, Fonts) • Includes/ (header.php, footer.php) • Uploads/ • .gitignore
api.invalid_csrf: Session expired, please reload the page
phonefake.title: PhoneFake
phonefake.tagline: Test your web apps in a realistic mobile frame — locally, with nothing to install.
phonefake.f1: 4 realistic devices: iPhone, Android, iPad, Android tablet — native dimensions.
phonefake.f2: Portrait ↔ landscape rotation in one click.
phonefake.f3: Compare two devices side by side.
phonefake.f4: Create a full PWA skeleton from the interface, with auto-generated icons.
phonefake.f5: 5 languages, accessibility options, and zero install — 100% local.
phonefake.not_installed: PhoneFake isn't installed here yet. Get it on GitHub and drop it into www/appli/:
menuedit.title: Menu editor
menuedit.intro: Add, edit, reorder or remove categories and links. Changes are saved to the menu file on this machine (local only).
menuedit.err_write: Could not write the menu file.
menuedit.confirm_delete_cat: Delete this category and all its links?
readme.f_menuedit: Built-in menu editor: add, edit, reorder categories and links (saved on this machine).
phonefake.where: How it works: each subfolder of www/appli/ that contains an index.html appears as a testable app inside PhoneFake.
menuedit.icon_hint: The icon is an Ionicons class (e.g. ion-bag, ion-code) shown next to the category.
